words.example: A teacher will accompany the students on the trip.
Please consider other people's feelings before you speak.

words.meaning: to include or deal with a subject or duty
words.example: The test will cover five chapters from the textbook.

words.meaning: to deal with a difficult situation
words.example: New students often face many changes.

words.meaning: to end something or decide something after thinking
words.example: The speaker concluded the talk with a short story.

words.meaning: to manage or deal with something
words.example: She can handle difficult customers politely.

words.meaning: to put something where it is held from above
words.example: The staff will hang the posters before the event.

words.meaning: to put something where it cannot be seen
words.example: Do not hide important information from your team.

words.meaning: to move something down or make it less
words.example: Please lower your voice in the library.

words.meaning: to offer to do something without being forced or paid
words.japaneseMeaning: ボランティアをする、志願する
words.example: Many students volunteer at the city festival.
